context: Tests whether the agent checks and preserves a known-good sibling deploy workflow instead of replacing it with a generic marketplace action or new shell.

checklist.name: Uses local composite action
checklist.description: The workflow deploys via `./.github/actions/cloudflare-pages-deploy` rather than a new marketplace deploy action

checklist.name: Composite matches sibling shape
checklist.description: The composite action uses Wrangler with `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` and `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` from env, matching the provided sibling pattern

checklist.name: Artifact pass-through
checklist.description: The deploy job downloads the artifact uploaded by the verify/build job and does not rebuild

checklist.name: E2E uses artifact
checklist.description: The e2e job also downloads the built artifact instead of running a separate build

checklist.name: Smoke after deploy
checklist.description: The smoke check runs after the deploy step and hits the deployed URL

checklist.name: Smoke fails closed
checklist.description: The smoke check uses `curl -fsS` or equivalent so non-2xx responses fail the job

checklist.name: No unexplained marketplace swap
checklist.description: The solution does not introduce a marketplace deploy action unless it explicitly explains why the provided sibling composite action does not fit

checklist.name: Mentions sibling precedent
checklist.description: The answer or generated docs mention that the workflow follows the existing `uinaf-console` deploy pattern

checklist.name: Uses narrow credentials
checklist.description: The workflow uses `vars.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` and `secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`, not hardcoded credentials

checklist.name: Deploy concurrency safe
checklist.description: The deploy job has non-cancellable concurrency for the production deploy target
